methods.name: login

tags.name: param
tags.text: options - Configuration object

tags.name: param
tags.text: options.accessToken - Twilio access token obtained from your backend server

tags.name: returns
tags.text: Promise that resolves with success status

tags.name: example

methods.docs:
Authenticate the user with Twilio Voice using an access token.

The access token should be generated on your backend server using your Twilio credentials.
This token is required to make and receive calls through Twilio Voice.

methods.name: logout

tags.name: returns
tags.text: Promise that resolves with success status

tags.name: example

methods.docs:
Log out the current user and unregister from Twilio Voice.

This will disconnect any active calls and stop the device from receiving
new incoming call notifications.

methods.name: isLoggedIn
methods.signature: () => Promise<{ isLoggedIn: boolean; hasValidToken: boolean; identity?: string; }>
methods.returns: Promise<{ isLoggedIn: boolean; hasValidToken: boolean; identity?: string | undefined; }>

tags.name: returns
tags.text: Promise that resolves with login status information

tags.name: returns
tags.text: isLoggedIn - Whether the user is currently logged in

tags.name: returns
tags.text: hasValidToken - Whether the access token is still valid

tags.name: returns
tags.text: identity - The user's Twilio identity (if logged in)

tags.name: example

methods.docs: Check if the user is currently logged in and has a valid access token.

methods.name: makeCall
methods.signature: (options: { to: string; displayName?: string; callerId?: string; }) => Promise<{ success: boolean; callSid?: string; }>

parameters.name: options
parameters.type: { to: string; displayName?: string | undefined; callerId?: string | undefined; }

methods.returns: Promise<{ success: boolean; callSid?: string | undefined; }>

tags.name: param
tags.text: options - Configuration object

tags.name: param
tags.text: options.to - Phone number (E.164 format) or Twilio client identifier to call

tags.name: param
tags.text:
options.displayName - Optional human-readable name used as the
iOS CXHandle value so Phone.app Recents renders a readable label
instead of the raw `to` identity.

tags.name: param
tags.text:
options.callerId - Optional caller ID/phone number to send to your
TwiML app so the backend can set the outbound `From` value instead of
defaulting to the contact URI.

tags.name: returns
tags.text: Promise that resolves with success status and call SID

tags.name: returns
tags.text: success - Whether the call was initiated successfully

tags.name: returns
tags.text: callSid - Unique identifier for this call (if successful)

tags.name: example

methods.docs:
Initiate an outgoing call to a phone number or client.

The user must be logged in before making a call. The call will be routed
through your Twilio backend configuration.

methods.name: acceptCall

tags.name: param
tags.text: options - Configuration object

tags.name: param
tags.text: options.callSid - Unique identifier of the call to accept

tags.name: returns
tags.text: Promise that resolves with success status

tags.name: example

methods.docs:
Accept an incoming call.

This should be called in response to a 'callInviteReceived' event.

methods.name: rejectCall

tags.name: param
tags.text: options - Configuration object

tags.name: param
tags.text: options.callSid - Unique identifier of the call to reject

tags.name: returns
tags.text: Promise that resolves with success status

tags.name: example

methods.docs:
Reject an incoming call.

This should be called in response to a 'callInviteReceived' event.
The caller will hear a busy signal or be directed to voicemail.

methods.name: endCall

tags.name: param
tags.text: options - Configuration object

tags.name: param
tags.text: options.callSid - Unique identifier of the call to end (optional, defaults to current active call)

tags.name: returns
tags.text: Promise that resolves with success status

tags.name: example

methods.docs:
End an active call.

If callSid is not provided, this will end the currently active call.

methods.name: muteCall
methods.signature: (options: { muted: boolean; callSid?: string; }) => Promise<{ success: boolean; }>

tags.name: param
tags.text: options - Configuration object

tags.name: param
tags.text: options.muted - Whether to mute (true) or unmute (false) the microphone

tags.name: param
tags.text: options.callSid - Unique identifier of the call (optional, defaults to current active call)

tags.name: returns
tags.text: Promise that resolves with success status

tags.name: example

methods.docs:
Mute or unmute the microphone during an active call.

When muted, the other party will not hear audio from your microphone.

methods.name: holdCall
methods.signature: (options: { hold: boolean; callSid?: string; }) => Promise<{ success: boolean; }>

tags.name: param
tags.text: options - Configuration object

tags.name: param
tags.text: options.hold - true to put the call on hold, false to take off hold

tags.name: param
tags.text: options.callSid - Unique identifier of the call (optional, defaults to current active call)

tags.name: returns
tags.text: Promise that resolves with success status

tags.name: example

methods.docs:
Place an active call on hold or take it off hold.

Hold pauses the call's media without disconnecting it. The remote party hears
silence (or hold music if configured server-side via TwiML); the local user
hears no audio. Re-issuing with `hold: false` resumes the call.

iOS: wraps `TVOCall.isOnHold = held` from the Twilio Voice iOS SDK.

methods.name: sendDigits
methods.signature: (options: { digits: string; callSid?: string; }) => Promise<{ success: boolean; }>

tags.name: param
tags.text: options - Configuration object

tags.name: param
tags.text: options.digits - String of DTMF characters to send (e.g., "1234", "*70#")

tags.name: param
tags.text: options.callSid - Unique identifier of the call (optional, defaults to current active call)

tags.name: returns
tags.text: Promise that resolves with success status

tags.name: example

methods.docs:
Send DTMF (touch-tone) digits on an active call.

Used to navigate IVR phone menus mid-call (e.g., "press 1 for billing").
Multiple digits can be sent in one call; characters supported are 0-9, *, #, w (wait).

iOS: wraps `TVOCall.sendDigits(_:)` from the Twilio Voice iOS SDK.

methods.name: setSpeaker

tags.name: param
tags.text: options - Configuration object

tags.name: param
tags.text: options.enabled - Whether to enable (true) or disable (false) speakerphone mode

tags.name: returns
tags.text: Promise that resolves with success status

tags.name: example

methods.docs:
Enable or disable speakerphone mode.

When enabled, audio will be routed through the device's speaker instead of the earpiece.

methods.name: getCallStatus
methods.signature: () => Promise<{ hasActiveCall: boolean; isOnHold: boolean; isMuted: boolean; callSid?: string; callState?: string; pendingInvites: CallInvite[]; activeCallsCount: number; }>
methods.returns: Promise<{ hasActiveCall: boolean; isOnHold: boolean; isMuted: boolean; callSid?: string | undefined; callState?: string | undefined; pendingInvites: CallInvite[]; activeCallsCount: number; }>

tags.name: returns
tags.text: Promise that resolves with call status information

tags.name: returns
tags.text: hasActiveCall - Whether there is currently an active call

tags.name: returns
tags.text: isOnHold - Whether the call is on hold

tags.name: returns
tags.text: isMuted - Whether the microphone is muted

tags.name: returns
tags.text: callSid - Unique identifier of the active call (if any)

tags.name: returns
tags.text: callState - Current state of the call (e.g., 'connecting', 'connected', 'ringing')

tags.name: returns
tags.text: pendingInvites - Array of pending incoming call invitations with the same data as callInviteReceived

tags.name: returns
tags.text: activeCallsCount - Total number of active calls being tracked

tags.name: example

methods.docs:
Get the current status of the active call.

This provides real-time information about the call state, mute status,
hold status, and call identifiers.

methods.name: checkMicrophonePermission

tags.name: returns
tags.text: Promise that resolves with permission status

tags.name: returns
tags.text: granted - Whether microphone permission has been granted

tags.name: example

methods.docs:
Check if microphone permission has been granted.

This does not request permission, only checks the current permission status.

methods.name: requestMicrophonePermission

tags.name: returns
tags.text: Promise that resolves with permission status

tags.name: returns
tags.text: granted - Whether microphone permission was granted

tags.name: example

methods.docs:
Request microphone permission from the user.

On iOS and Android, this will show the system permission dialog if permission
has not been granted yet. If permission was previously denied, the user may need
to grant it in system settings.

methods.name: addListener

parameters.name: listenerFunc
parameters.docs: - Callback function to handle the event

tags.name: param
tags.text: eventName - The event name ('callInviteReceived')

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc - Callback function to handle the event

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data - Event data of type {@link CallInvite}

tags.name: returns
tags.text: Promise that resolves with a listener handle for removing the listener

tags.name: example

methods.docs:
Listen for incoming call invitations.

This event is fired when another user or phone number is calling you.
You should call acceptCall() or rejectCall() in response.

methods.name: addListener

parameters.name: listenerFunc
parameters.docs: - Callback function to handle the event

tags.name: param
tags.text: eventName - The event name ('callConnected')

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc - Callback function to handle the event

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data - Event data

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data.callSid - Unique identifier for the connected call

tags.name: returns
tags.text: Promise that resolves with a listener handle for removing the listener

tags.name: example

methods.docs:
Listen for call connected events.

This event is fired when a call (incoming or outgoing) has been successfully
connected and audio can be heard.

methods.name: addListener
methods.signature: (eventName: 'callInviteCancelled', listenerFunc: (data: { callSid: string; reason: 'user_declined' | 'remote_cancelled'; }) => void) => Promise<PluginListenerHandle>

parameters.name: listenerFunc
parameters.docs: - Callback function to handle the event

tags.name: param
tags.text: eventName - The event name ('callInviteCancelled')

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc - Callback function to handle the event

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data - Event data

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data.callSid - Unique identifier for the cancelled call

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data.reason - Reason for cancellation ('user_declined' or 'remote_cancelled')

tags.name: returns
tags.text: Promise that resolves with a listener handle for removing the listener

tags.name: example

methods.docs:
Listen for call invite cancellation events.

This event is fired when an incoming call invitation is cancelled before being
answered, either by the caller hanging up or by the user declining.

methods.name: addListener
methods.signature: (eventName: 'outgoingCallInitiated', listenerFunc: (data: { callSid: string; to: string; source: 'app' | 'system'; displayName?: string; }) => void) => Promise<PluginListenerHandle>

parameters.name: listenerFunc
parameters.docs: - Callback function to handle the event
parameters.type: (data: { callSid: string; to: string; source: 'app' | 'system'; displayName?: string | undefined; }) => void

tags.name: param
tags.text: eventName - The event name ('outgoingCallInitiated')

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc - Callback function to handle the event

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data - Event data

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data.callSid - Unique identifier for the outgoing call

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data.to - Phone number or client identifier being called

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data.source - Source of the call ('app' or 'system')

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data.displayName - Display name for the recipient (optional)

tags.name: returns
tags.text: Promise that resolves with a listener handle for removing the listener

tags.name: example

methods.docs:
Listen for outgoing call initiation events.

This event is fired when an outgoing call is initiated, either from the app
or from the system (e.g., CallKit on iOS, Telecom on Android).

methods.name: addListener
methods.signature: (eventName: 'outgoingCallFailed', listenerFunc: (data: { callSid: string; to: string; reason: 'missing_access_token' | 'connection_failed' | 'no_call_details' | 'microphone_permission_denied' | 'invalid_contact' | 'callkit_request_failed' | 'unsupported_intent'; displayName?: string; }) => void) => Promise<PluginListenerHandle>

parameters.name: listenerFunc
parameters.docs: - Callback function to handle the event
parameters.type: (data: { callSid: string; to: string; reason: 'missing_access_token' | 'connection_failed' | 'no_call_details' | 'microphone_permission_denied' | 'invalid_contact' | 'callkit_request_failed' | 'unsupported_intent'; displayName?: string | undefined; }) => void

tags.name: param
tags.text: eventName - The event name ('outgoingCallFailed')

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc - Callback function to handle the event

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data - Event data

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data.callSid - Unique identifier for the failed call

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data.to - Phone number or client identifier that was being called

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data.reason - Reason for the failure

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data.displayName - Display name for the recipient (optional)

tags.name: returns
tags.text: Promise that resolves with a listener handle for removing the listener

tags.name: example

methods.docs:
Listen for outgoing call failure events.

This event is fired when an outgoing call fails to connect due to various reasons
such as missing credentials, permission issues, or network problems.

methods.docs:
Listen for call disconnection events.

This event is fired when a call ends, either normally or due to an error.

Listen for call ringing events.

This event is fired when an outgoing call starts ringing on the other end.

methods.name: addListener
methods.signature: (eventName: 'callReconnecting', listenerFunc: (data: { callSid: string; error?: string; }) => void) => Promise<PluginListenerHandle>

parameters.name: listenerFunc
parameters.docs: - Callback function to handle the event
parameters.type: (data: { callSid: string; error?: string | undefined; }) => void

tags.name: param
tags.text: eventName - The event name ('callReconnecting')

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc - Callback function to handle the event

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data - Event data

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data.callSid - Unique identifier for the reconnecting call

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data.error - Error message describing the connection issue (optional)

tags.name: returns
tags.text: Promise that resolves with a listener handle for removing the listener

tags.name: example

methods.docs:
Listen for call reconnecting events.

This event is fired when a call loses connection and Twilio is attempting to
reconnect. The call is not disconnected yet but audio may be interrupted.

methods.name: addListener

parameters.name: listenerFunc
parameters.docs: - Callback function to handle the event

tags.name: param
tags.text: eventName - The event name ('callReconnected')

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc - Callback function to handle the event

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data - Event data

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data.callSid - Unique identifier for the reconnected call

tags.name: returns
tags.text: Promise that resolves with a listener handle for removing the listener

tags.name: example

methods.docs:
Listen for call reconnected events.

This event is fired when a call successfully reconnects after a connection loss.
Audio should resume normally after this event.

methods.name: addListener
methods.signature: (eventName: 'callQualityWarningsChanged', listenerFunc: (data: { callSid: string; currentWarnings: string[]; previousWarnings: string[]; }) => void) => Promise<PluginListenerHandle>

parameters.name: listenerFunc
parameters.docs: - Callback function to handle the event
parameters.type: (data: { callSid: string; currentWarnings: string[]; previousWarnings: string[]; }) => void

tags.name: param
tags.text: eventName - The event name ('callQualityWarningsChanged')

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc - Callback function to handle the event

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data - Event data

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data.callSid - Unique identifier for the call

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data.currentWarnings - Array of current quality warnings

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data.previousWarnings - Array of previous quality warnings

tags.name: returns
tags.text: Promise that resolves with a listener handle for removing the listener

tags.name: example

methods.docs:
Listen for call quality warning events.

This event is fired when the call quality changes, providing warnings about
potential issues like high jitter, packet loss, or low audio levels.

methods.name: addListener

parameters.name: listenerFunc
parameters.docs: - Callback function to handle the event

tags.name: param
tags.text: eventName - The event name ('registrationSuccess')

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc - Callback function to handle the event

tags.name: returns
tags.text: Promise that resolves with a listener handle for removing the listener

tags.name: example

methods.docs:
Listen for successful registration events.

This event is fired when the device successfully registers with Twilio Voice
and is ready to make and receive calls. This typically occurs after a successful
login with a valid access token.

methods.name: addListener

parameters.name: listenerFunc
parameters.docs: - Callback function to handle the event

tags.name: param
tags.text: eventName - The event name ('registrationFailure')

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc - Callback function to handle the event

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data - Event data

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc.data.error - Error message describing the registration failure

tags.name: returns
tags.text: Promise that resolves with a listener handle for removing the listener

tags.name: example

methods.docs:
Listen for registration failure events.

This event is fired when the device fails to register with Twilio Voice,
typically due to an invalid or expired access token, network issues, or
Twilio service problems.

methods.name: removeAllListeners

tags.name: returns
tags.text: Promise that resolves when all listeners have been removed

tags.name: example

methods.docs:
Remove all registered event listeners.

This is useful for cleanup when your component unmounts or when you want to
reset all event handling.

methods.name: getPluginVersion

tags.name: returns
tags.text: a Promise with version for this plugin

tags.name: throws
tags.text: An error if something went wrong

methods.docs: Get the native Capacitor plugin version

interfaces.name: CallInvite
interfaces.docs:
Represents a pending incoming call invitation.

This interface describes the data structure for call invitations that have been received
but not yet accepted or rejected. The same structure is used both in the
`callInviteReceived` event and in the `pendingInvites` array returned by `getCallStatus()`.

tags.name: example

properties.name: callSid
properties.docs: Unique identifier for the incoming call invitation

properties.name: from
properties.docs: Phone number or client identifier of the caller (may include custom caller name)

properties.name: to
properties.docs: Phone number or client identifier being called

properties.name: customParams
properties.docs: Custom parameters passed with the call invitation

typeAliases.name: Record
typeAliases.docs: Construct a type with a set of properties K of type T
